expect: PHIL-15 is FALSE because >=20% of encountered inputs (signals, domains, human questions) are neither integrated nor analyzed — selection bias not universal application
actual: PARTIAL FALSIFICATION. Initial encounter processing is near-universal (98.6% signal rate, 95.7% HQ rate). But sustained application is selective: 27.3% of domains have zero active frontiers, 31.7% of knowledge is DECAYED, 67% of prescriptions are ASPIRATIONAL (unenforced). The swarm encounters its own prescriptions and doesn't apply itself to them — self-referential falsification.
diff: Expected binary FALSE, found temporal split: universality holds for first-contact but fails for sustained engagement. PHIL-15's present-tense claim ('applies itself') requires ongoing application, not just initial encounter. 27% domain abandonment + 67% prescription gap = selective, not universal.

signal_processing.note: many closures claim absorbed without behavioral evidence

self_apply: This falsification was selective — tested PHIL-15 because dispatch ranked it high, not because it was the hardest target. PHIL-14 (all 12 metrics self-referential per S477) might be a harder falsification. Protocol vulnerability: dispatch-driven falsification tests what's convenient, not what's most resistant.
sample_size: n=1500 knowledge items, 71 signals, 44 domains, 65 lanes, 308 prescriptions
